title: Data Deduplication
concept: Deduplicating training text removes repetitive pages, preventing the model from memorizing duplicate strings and wasting training compute.

exercises.prompt: Why is it bad for an AI model to read duplicate web pages during its training?

options.text: It wastes computer power and causes the AI to memorize text word-for-word.
It makes the AI run much faster on your phone.
It helps the AI learn to write code in multiple languages.

exercises.explain: Training on the same text over and over wastes compute time and causes the model to memorize specific sentences instead of learning how language works.

cards.title: Too Much of a Good Thing
cards.body: Have you ever read a book where the same paragraph was printed over and over again on every single page? You would get bored quickly, and you wouldn't learn anything new. AI models face the same issue! If we feed them repeated text, they waste energy and get stuck repeating themselves. Let's learn how we clean up this clutter!

cards.title: What is Deduplication?
cards.body: Data deduplication is the process of scanning your massive training dataset and removing duplicate copies of pages, articles, or paragraphs. By keeping only unique data, we save valuable training time and help the model learn general concepts instead of memorizing specific paragraphs.

cards.title: The Flashcard Stack
cards.body: Imagine your AI is studying for a big exam using a stack of flashcards. If someone sneaks in ten identical copies of the exact same flashcard, the AI will keep reading the same fact over and over. It wastes time it could spend learning new things, and it might end up just memorizing that one card word-for-word! Deduplication is like sorting through the stack beforehand and throwing out the duplicate cards.

cards.title: Deduplication in Action
cards.body: Web scrapers often download the exact same Terms of Service page, spam article, or standard header from thousands of different websites. By running a deduplication script, we can condense 1,000 identical pages of legal boilerplate into just 1 single page before feeding the text to our model.

cards.title: Myth: More Repetition is Always Better
cards.body: Many think that showing the AI the exact same text millions of times makes it smarter. In reality, too much repetition causes the model to memorize sentences word-for-word. When asked a question, it might just spit out the memorized spam instead of generating a creative, helpful answer!

cards.title: Before vs. After Deduplication
cards.body: Look at how cleaning up our training data makes it much more efficient and diverse for the AI to learn from.

right.text: Only one high-quality copy of the article is kept for training.

left.text: The same news article copied 50 times across different spam websites.

exercises.prompt: What happens if we train a large language model on data that has NOT been deduplicated?

options.text: The model gets confused and forgets English.
The model wastes training compute on repetitive text and memorizes duplicate strings.
The dataset becomes too small for the model to learn anything at all.
The model will only be able to output numbers.

exercises.explain: Deduplication keeps only unique instances of data, reducing the overall size of the training dataset and preventing wasted computational power.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Data deduplication helps prevent an AI model from wasting energy and computing power on repetitive text.
exercises.explain: Deduplication helps the model learn general concepts instead of memorizing specific files, and it saves valuable compute resources by not processing the same data twice.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of why we remove duplicate text.
exercises.sentence: If we do not remove duplicate pages, the model might simply memorise the text word-for-word.
exercises.explain: When an AI sees the exact same text too many times, it tends to memorize it word-for-word instead of learning the underlying concepts.

exercises.prompt: An AI model was trained on messy web data containing millions of duplicated pages. Which of these outputs is most likely a result of that bad training data when asked to write a story?

options.text: Once upon a time in a digital forest, a little blue bird discovered a glowing key hidden inside an old hollow oak tree.
Once upon a time time time time in a digital digital digital forest forest forest forest...
In a magical digital forest, a small rabbit hopped along the glowing paths searching for its lost friend.

exercises.explain: The first prompt asks for a creative, unique story. The output in Option B is a repetitive loop, which is a classic sign of a model that has memorized repetitive data patterns.

exercises.prompt: Why is data deduplication considered an essential step before training a large language model?

options.text: It makes the AI memorize specific sentences perfectly so it never forgets them.
It increases the total size of the training dataset to make the model bigger.
It prevents the AI from wasting computing power on repeating data and reduces rote memorization.
It translates all the text into a single common language automatically.

exercises.explain: Deduplication removes identical or highly similar text from the dataset. This frees up precious compute time to learn a wider variety of concepts, and stops the model from memorizing text patterns too rigidly.
